description: A write updates the store but readers keep the old cached value, or a write-behind entry is lost on crash.
body: Failure mode: with write-behind or a missed invalidation, a write updates the store while readers keep getting the old cached value, or an unflushed write-behind entry is lost on crash. Detection signals: users see their own update missing on reload; reconciliation finds cache and store disagree; lost writes after a restart. Do not use write-behind for data that must survive a crash. Safer fallback: invalidate or update the key synchronously on write, add a backstop TTL, and use write-through where durability matters.
